task_title: Annotate slide 6 with blue directional arrows
instruction: Open slide 6 and add a large down arrow above the left image with a blue gradient fill.

window_merge_decisions.reason: The next step directly continues configuring the same first gradient stop color: it starts from the palette opened in the previous step and proceeds to the custom color dialog for that same control.

The next step directly continues the same gradient-stop color edit: the palette opened in s20 is the visible starting state in s21, and s21 simply drills into the custom color picker for that same second stop.

task_goal: The target slide is active and the first annotation arrow is placed, positioned, and styled correctly above the left image.

action_preconditions: The presentation is open in editing mode and the Slides pane is visible.

nl_position: the thumbnail of slide 6 in the left Slides pane

steps.action_before_state: A different slide is active while slide 6 is visible in the thumbnail list.
action_after_effects: Slide 6 becomes the active slide in the main canvas.
steps.nl_explanation: Click slide 6 to start editing the slide that needs arrow annotations.

action_preconditions: Slide 6 is active and the main menu bar is visible.

nl_position: the Insert label in the top menu bar

steps.action_before_state: The slide is open with no top menu expanded.
action_after_effects: The Insert menu opens.
steps.nl_explanation: Open the Insert menu to access shape tools.

action_preconditions: The Insert menu is open.

nl_position: the Shape item in the Insert dropdown menu

steps.action_before_state: The Insert dropdown is visible with several object types listed.
action_after_effects: The Shape submenu opens.
steps.nl_explanation: Choose Shape from the Insert menu to reach the arrow categories.

action_preconditions: The Shape submenu is open.

nl_position: the Block Arrows item in the Shape submenu

steps.action_before_state: The Shape submenu lists categories such as Basic Shapes and Block Arrows.
action_after_effects: The Block Arrows submenu opens.
steps.nl_explanation: Open the Block Arrows category to pick a directional arrow.

action_preconditions: The Block Arrows submenu is open.

nl_position: the Down Arrow item in the Block Arrows submenu

steps.action_before_state: The arrow shape submenu is visible with options like Right Arrow, Left Arrow, and Down Arrow.
action_after_effects: The menu closes and the cursor enters down-arrow drawing mode.
steps.nl_explanation: Select the Down Arrow shape so it can be drawn onto the slide.

action_preconditions: The Down Arrow tool is active on slide 6.
steps.action_before_state: The slide shows the podium photo and text with empty space above the left image.
action_after_effects: A down arrow is drawn above the left image and remains selected.
steps.nl_explanation: Drag on the slide to place the first down arrow above the podium image.

action_preconditions: The new down arrow is selected.
steps.action_before_state: The down arrow sits above the left image with resize handles visible.
action_after_effects: The selected arrow shifts slightly to the left.
steps.nl_explanation: Press Left to nudge the selected arrow toward the intended position.

steps.step_goal: Move the arrow slightly left again
action_preconditions: The down arrow is still selected after one left nudge.
steps.action_before_state: The arrow remains selected above the left image.
action_after_effects: The selected arrow moves a little further left.
steps.nl_explanation: Press Left again to refine the arrow placement.

action_preconditions: The first down arrow is selected and its properties are visible in the sidebar.

nl_position: the fill color swatch in the Area section of the right Properties sidebar

steps.action_before_state: The selected arrow shows a solid fill setting in the Area section.
action_after_effects: The fill control is activated for editing.
steps.nl_explanation: Click the fill control to start changing the arrow appearance.

action_preconditions: The fill type dropdown is open in the Area section.

nl_position: the Gradient option in the Fill type dropdown

steps.action_before_state: The Fill type menu is open with options including None, Color, and Gradient.
action_after_effects: The arrow fill changes to Gradient and gradient controls appear.
steps.nl_explanation: Choose Gradient so the arrow can use a two-color fill.

action_preconditions: Gradient controls are visible for the selected arrow.

nl_position: the 30° angle field in the gradient controls on the right sidebar

steps.action_before_state: The gradient angle is shown as 30°.
action_after_effects: The angle field becomes active for editing.
steps.nl_explanation: Activate the gradient angle field to replace its value.

steps.step_goal: Place the cursor in the angle value
action_preconditions: The gradient angle field is active.

nl_position: the active 30° angle input box in the gradient controls

steps.action_before_state: The 30° value is highlighted inside the angle field.
action_after_effects: The text cursor is positioned in the angle field, ready for typing.
steps.nl_explanation: Click in the angle box to prepare the current value for replacement.

action_preconditions: The gradient angle field is active and ready for text input.
steps.action_before_state: The current angle value is selected in the input box.
action_after_effects: The angle field now contains 180.
steps.nl_explanation: Type 180 to reverse the gradient direction.

action_preconditions: The angle field contains the new value 180.
steps.action_before_state: The gradient angle input shows 180 but has not yet been confirmed.
action_after_effects: The 180° angle is applied to the selected arrow.
steps.nl_explanation: Press Enter to commit the new gradient angle.

steps.step_goal: Open the first gradient stop palette
action_preconditions: The selected arrow uses a gradient and both stop colors are visible in the sidebar.

nl_position: the top gradient color swatch labeled Light Green 4 in the Area section

steps.action_before_state: The first gradient stop is still set to a light green preset.
action_after_effects: The color palette for the first gradient stop opens.
steps.nl_explanation: Open the first gradient color control to replace the default green.

action_preconditions: The first-stop palette is open with preset colors and a Custom Color option.

nl_position: the Custom Color option at the bottom of the open color palette

steps.action_before_state: A preset color palette is visible for the first gradient stop.
action_after_effects: The Pick a Color dialog opens for the first gradient stop.
steps.nl_explanation: Open the custom color dialog to enter an exact blue value for the first gradient stop.

steps.step_goal: Select the existing first-stop hex value
action_preconditions: The Pick a Color dialog is open for the first gradient stop.

nl_position: the Hex # input field in the Pick a Color dialog

steps.action_before_state: The dialog shows the current first-stop hex value in the Hex # field.
action_after_effects: The existing hex value is selected for replacement.
steps.nl_explanation: Select the current hex code so it can be overwritten with the desired blue.

steps.step_goal: Type the darker blue hex code
action_preconditions: The Hex # field for the first gradient stop is active.
steps.action_before_state: The existing hex code is selected in the Pick a Color dialog.
action_after_effects: The Hex # field now contains 3e7fcc and the preview shifts to blue.
steps.nl_explanation: Type 3e7fcc to set the first gradient stop to a darker blue.

action_preconditions: The first-stop Hex # field contains 3e7fcc.
steps.action_before_state: The new blue hex value is entered in the dialog but not yet confirmed.
action_after_effects: The first custom color is committed in the color dialog.
steps.nl_explanation: Press Enter to apply the first blue custom color.

steps.step_goal: Open the second gradient stop palette
action_preconditions: The arrow properties are visible again with the first stop set to #3E7FCC.

nl_position: the lower gradient color swatch labeled Light Red 4 in the Area section

steps.action_before_state: The second gradient stop is still set to a light red preset.
action_after_effects: The color palette for the second gradient stop opens.
steps.nl_explanation: Open the second gradient color control to replace the default red.

action_preconditions: The second-stop palette is open with preset colors and a Custom Color option.

nl_position: the Custom Color option at the bottom of the open color palette

steps.action_before_state: A preset color palette is visible for the second gradient stop.
action_after_effects: The Pick a Color dialog opens for the second gradient stop.
steps.nl_explanation: Open the custom color dialog to enter an exact light blue value for the second gradient stop.

steps.step_goal: Select the existing second-stop hex value
action_preconditions: The Pick a Color dialog is open for the second gradient stop.

nl_position: the Hex # input field in the Pick a Color dialog

steps.action_before_state: The dialog shows the current second-stop hex value ffd7d7.
action_after_effects: The existing second-stop hex value is selected for replacement.
steps.nl_explanation: Select the current second-stop hex code so it can be replaced.

steps.step_goal: Type the lighter blue hex code
action_preconditions: The Hex # field for the second gradient stop is active.
steps.action_before_state: The current second-stop hex code is selected in the dialog.
action_after_effects: The Hex # field now contains a4c1ff and the preview becomes a lighter blue.
steps.nl_explanation: Type a4c1ff to set the second gradient stop to a lighter blue.

action_preconditions: The second-stop Hex # field contains a4c1ff.
steps.action_before_state: The new light-blue hex value is entered in the dialog but not yet confirmed.
action_after_effects: The second custom color is committed in the color dialog.
steps.nl_explanation: Press Enter to apply the second blue custom color.

action_preconditions: The first arrow remains selected on slide 6.

nl_position: a blank white area near the upper-right of the slide canvas

steps.action_before_state: The down arrow above the left image is selected with resize handles visible.
action_after_effects: The arrow is deselected and its selection handles disappear.
steps.nl_explanation: Click empty slide space to finish editing the first arrow.
